Guidelines.title: Content Guidelines for Creators
Guidelines.subtitle: What you can publish on EarnLumens and what we avoid
Guidelines.intro: EarnLumens is a financial education platform with a focus on the Stellar ecosystem. We want you to publish freely: these rules exist only to prevent fraud, harmful content, and spam, not to limit your creativity. If your content is honest, valuable, and not misleading, it will almost always be approved.

sections.heading: 1. What you can publish freely
sections.body: The range of acceptable topics is broad. You do not need permission or a rigid format:
items: Education about the Stellar network (protocol, SCP, Soroban, SDKs, anchors, DEX) and XLM
General financial education: saving, budgeting, debt, responsible investing, taxes
Practical tutorials, code demos, wallet and dApp walkthroughs
Honest comparisons between networks, wallets, or protocols, including ones outside Stellar, as long as the comparison is informative
Personal opinions and analysis clearly identified as opinion
Content in any language supported by the platform

sections.heading: 2. What we always reject
sections.body: These categories are rejected without exception because they put the community at risk or are illegal:
items: Ponzi schemes, pyramid schemes, pump and dump, rug pulls, or guaranteed-return promises
Paid trading signals, buy/sell recommendations with specific amounts, or personalized financial advice
Phishing, fake airdrops, requests for private keys, seed phrases, or credentials
Impersonation of public figures, projects, or EarnLumens / SDF itself
Hate speech, harassment, discrimination, or incitement to violence
Explicit sexual content, graphic violence, or material involving minors
Clearly copyrighted material without authorization (music, video, books, software)
Personal data of third parties without their consent
Obvious spam: nonsensical titles, mass-duplicated content, blank screens, AI content without human review

sections.heading: 3. Gray area: may go to manual review
sections.body: These cases are not automatically rejected, but they may be reviewed by a human moderator:
items: Mentioning specific cryptocurrencies or tokens without clear educational context
Promotion of your own products or services without labeling them as sponsored
Claims about past performance without a verifiable source
Very short content or low technical quality that makes its value hard to assess

sections.heading: 4. Best practices (recommended, not required)
sections.body: These are not approval requirements, but they help your content be better received and reduce moderation friction:
items: Use a descriptive title without misleading clickbait
Cite sources when using data, statistics, or figures
Clarify that the content is educational and not personalized financial advice
Mention it if you hold a significant position in an asset you discuss
Label sponsored or affiliate content
For Stellar wallets, DEXs, and tools, we prefer the ones listed at https://stellar.org/ecosystem as a reliable reference, but you may mention others as long as you are not promoting fraud

Guidelines.footer: These guidelines may be updated. The goal is a safe educational space: we reject harmful content, not different ideas. If you believe a rejection was unfair, you can revise and resubmit, or request manual review from the tenant team.
